source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/06_인허가 조사 및 보고자료/230906 - 2G_Aspenstate_Test report 추가발급/3.1 Risk Management Plan.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents the manufacturer's risk management plan, including scope, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, verification, and production/post-production information review.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.
The product is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, and is not to be used for mammography.
The document defines probability, severity, risk matrix, acceptable and unacceptable risk regions, and overall residual risk evaluation.

Production and post-production information is collected from internal sources such as PMCF, complaints, CAPA reports, and service records, and external sources such as recalls, side effect reports, literature, and similar device information.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities, identifying and describing the medical device and the life cycle phases for which each element of the plan is applicable;

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: We define and document the policy for determining criteria for risk acceptability;

concepts.name: Risk evaluation matrix
concepts.evidence: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Post-production Information should be collected through the collection methods such as surveys, customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaints (Internet, telephone, etc.), or articles or news reports.

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up)

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The model names are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The revision number shown in the revision history is 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision History Revision No. Rev. Date Revision Description 0 Establishment

evidence.claim: The plan is established by H&abyz Co., Ltd. in accordance with the risk management procedure.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all life-cycle stages of the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The device classification stated in the plan is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life stated in the plan is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors

evidence.claim: The detector supports auto exposure detection technology for use without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The European Union MDR is listed as an applicable national or regional regulation.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: EN ISO 14971:2019 is listed as a relevant standard for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable regulations, relevant international standards, state of the art, and known stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 through 4 are acceptable, and risk levels 5 through 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Unacceptable risk policy is project cancellation or device redesign.
evidence.evidenceText: Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable risk Project cancellation or device redesign

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after all risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

evidence.claim: If overall residual risk is acceptable, users are informed of significant residual risks in accompanying documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: If the overall residual risk is judged acceptable, the manufacturer shall inform users of significant residual risks and shall include the necessary information in the accompanying documentation in order to disclose those residual risks.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information includes internal PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information includes recalls, side effect reports, literature, expert opinions, similar medical device information, and law or standard trends.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected production and post-production information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the source text; the prepared, reviewed, approved date fields and revision date field are blank.
Authority signal metadata says FDA, but the source text lists European Union regulation and Korean medical device regulation references; FDA is not supported by the source text.
Some extracted text contains spacing, grammar, and OCR/preprocessing artifacts such as concatenated words and typographical errors, but key regulatory content remains readable.
